message:
fix(tui): emit unterminated code fences as CodeBlock for streaming syntax highlighting

The markdown fenced-code-block parser previously treated an unterminated
fence (no closing ```) as an error and fell back to returning the entire
input as a single MarkdownChunk::Text. Now it emits the unterminated
fence as a valid MarkdownChunk::CodeBlock, so during streaming (when
the closing ``` hasn't arrived yet), partially-received code blocks
still get syntax highlighting and don't destroy previously-rendered
chunks.

Test split_markdown_fenced_code_blocks_unterminated_fence updated to
expect new behavior: 2 chunks (Text + CodeBlock) instead of 1 (Text),
with assertions verifying language and code content of the code block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
